seoTitle: How to Use Zapier with Airtable: Connect & Integrate
metaDescription: Connect Airtable to Zapier using a Personal Access Token, set up triggers on new records, and map data to apps like Gmail or Slack.
intro: Zapier extends what Airtable can do by connecting it to hundreds of other apps, triggered whenever a record is created or updated. This guide covers generating the Personal Access Token Zapier needs to authenticate, setting up your first trigger, and mapping data into a third-party action.

steps.heading: Start a new Zap
steps.body: Open your Zapier dashboard and select 'Create a Zap' to begin building the automation.

steps.heading: Generate an Airtable Personal Access Token
steps.body: Go to your Airtable Developer Hub and generate a secure Personal Access Token to authenticate the connection.

steps.heading: Authenticate and select your base
steps.body: Connect Airtable inside Zapier using the token, then select your target base.

steps.heading: Define your trigger
steps.body: Choose a trigger event like New Record, and pull test data from Airtable to confirm the connection works.

steps.heading: Add an action and map fields
steps.body: Add an action step and map your Airtable fields to a third-party application such as Gmail, Slack, or Google Sheets.

steps.heading: Test and publish
steps.body: Run a live integration test to verify data transfers correctly, then click Publish to activate the Zap.

faq.question: What do I need from Airtable to connect it to Zapier?
faq.answer: You need a Personal Access Token generated from Airtable's Developer Hub, which Zapier uses to authenticate the connection.

faq.question: What triggers can I use from Airtable in Zapier?
faq.answer: Common triggers include new record creation or updates to existing records within a specified base and table.

faq.question: Does Zapier have usage limits for Airtable automations?
faq.answer: Yes, Zapier's free and paid plans have task limits, so it's worth monitoring your Zap history to track usage over time.

faq.question: Can I connect Airtable to multiple apps in one Zap?
faq.answer: Yes, a single Zap can include multiple action steps, sending Airtable data to more than one destination app in sequence.
